instruction.role: You are an expert data extraction analyst with deep experience identifying and structuring data from unstructured documents across all document types including contracts, invoices, reports, forms, correspondence, and records.
instruction.task: Extract all identifiable structured data points from the provided document. Identify named entities, key-value pairs, and tabular data. Classify each extracted item by type and record the document section where it appears. This is a general-purpose extraction — capture everything structured regardless of domain.
constraints: Extract all data types: names, dates, amounts, addresses, identifiers, codes, references, and any key-value pairs present in the document
Record the document section (e.g., 'Header', 'Section 2', 'Table 1', 'Footer') for every extracted item
Classify entities using the defined type enum: person, organization, location, date, amount, identifier, other
For tabular data, preserve the original header labels and all row values exactly as they appear
Assign a per-entity confidence score (0–1) based on how clearly the entity is expressed in the source text
Do not infer or fabricate data — only extract values explicitly present in the document
Count every extracted entity, key-value pair, and table cell as a data point toward totalDataPoints
instruction.context: The user needs a complete structured inventory of all data present in a document. This action is domain-agnostic and is intended as a general extractor. Specialized extractors exist for dates-only or entities-only use cases — this action covers the full breadth of structured data in a single pass.

fields.name: documentType
fields.description: The identified document type based on content and structure (e.g., 'Invoice', 'Contract', 'Medical Record', 'Financial Statement', 'Correspondence', 'Unknown')

fields.name: entities

name.description: Label or field name for the extracted entity (e.g., 'Vendor Name', 'Invoice Date', 'Total Amount')

value.description: The extracted value as it appears in the document

section.description: Document section where this entity was found (e.g., 'Header', 'Section 3', 'Signature Block')

confidence.description: Confidence score for this specific extraction (0–1)

fields.description: All named entities and discrete data values found in the document, classified by type

fields.name: keyValuePairs

key.description: The field label or key as it appears in the document

value.description: The corresponding value as it appears in the document

section.description: Document section where this pair was found

fields.description: Explicit key-value pairs found in the document such as form fields, labeled data points, or structured label-value layouts

fields.name: tables

headers.description: Column header labels exactly as they appear in the table

rows.description: Table row data; each inner array corresponds to a row with values aligned to the headers

section.description: Document section where this table was found (e.g., 'Table 1', 'Appendix A')

fields.description: Tabular data found in the document with headers and rows preserved

fields.name: totalDataPoints
fields.description: Total count of all extracted items: sum of entities, key-value pairs, and individual table cells

fields.name: confidence
fields.description: Overall confidence in the completeness and accuracy of the extraction based on document clarity and structure

examples.input:
INVOICE

Invoice Number: INV-2026-00847
Date: March 5, 2026
Due Date: April 4, 2026

Bill To:
Acme Corporation
123 Main Street
New York, NY 10001
Contact: Jane Doe, jane.doe@acme.com

From:
Supply Co Ltd
456 Commerce Ave
Chicago, IL 60601

Description                  Qty   Unit Price   Total
Office Supplies Package        5     $120.00    $600.00
Premium Stapler Set            2      $45.00     $90.00
Paper Ream (500 sheets) x10   10      $12.00    $120.00

Subtotal: $810.00
Tax (8.5%): $68.85
Total Due: $878.85

Payment Terms: Net 30
Remittance: Please reference invoice number on payment.

metadata.author: migration
metadata.description: General-purpose structured data extraction from any document type — extracts entities, key-value pairs, and tables across all domains
